goal: Agent language rule: seal/append/pr-description match the user's conversation language

summary.title: Agent language rule: seal/append/pr-description match the user's conversation language
summary.what: Add a load-bearing language rule to agent guidance: when the user writes to the agent in Chinese, the agent seals in Chinese; English in, English out; mixed → match dominant language. Lands in three docs: agents-md.md (v12→v13, full section near the top), agents-stub.md (v8→v9, one-paragraph reminder), skills/mainline/SKILL.md (full section above Trigger Policy). Code identifiers, command names, file paths stay in their original form regardless of conversation language.
summary.why: The seal record is the team's long-term memory. A teammate reading mainline show <id> six months later has to recognise the work as theirs. Translating a Chinese task into an English seal makes the corpus harder to read for the people whose memory it is — and silently degrades the same intent-first thesis the rest of the system is built around.

decisions.chose: top of agents-md (after v0.3 invariant block, before workflow steps); top of SKILL.md (above Trigger Policy); short reminder in stub
decisions.rationale: load-bearing rules go near the top so a quick-reading agent sees them on first contact; the stub is a pointer not a full doc, so a one-paragraph reminder is enough

decisions.chose: explicitly tell agents not to translate the user's start goal
decisions.rationale: the goal becomes the headline in mainline log for everyone; paraphrasing means everyone re-translates

decisions.chose: explicitly carve out code names / commands / paths from the rule
decisions.rationale: otherwise an over-eager agent might localise mainline seal --submit or rename a Go function name

risks: A user who actually wants the seal in English while writing in Chinese (e.g. for a global team) is now harder to satisfy — they have to ask explicitly. Acceptable: explicit ask is the right channel for that override.

anti_patterns.what: Translating the user's mainline start goal text into another language
anti_patterns.why: the goal is the headline in mainline log; everyone reads the original verbatim; translating means everyone re-derives the user's intent

anti_patterns.what: Localising code identifiers / command names / file paths into the user's natural language
anti_patterns.why: breaks copy-paste runnability and search; the technical substrate is the same regardless of conversation language

followups: Audit pr-description output (engine/pr_description.go) to ensure it reuses the seal text as-is rather than re-rendering — otherwise the language rule is undone at PR-description generation time
Consider a seal-time lint warning if the seal title language differs sharply from the active draft's goal language (low-priority; current rule is doc-only)
